responseMessageContents.text:
The system context says this is a NEW empty project, but I just cloned a repo into it. The repo has a rork.json with three apps already defined (swift, react-native, supabase). The user asked to clone the repo, which I did. Let me verify the structure is properly set up and report back.

The system context seems to conflict with reality - the project is no longer empty since I just cloned the repo. I should report what I did and the state of the project.

Done! I cloned `ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest` into your Rork workspace. Here's what's now in place:

**Project structure** (from `rork.json`):
- **`ios-ivx`** — Swift app ("Ivx")
- **`expo`** — React Native app ("IVX AI")
- **`backend`** — Supabase backend

**Other notable files:** `package.json` (ivx-holdings-platform), `Dockerfile`, `render.yaml`, `DEPLOYMENT.md`, `deploy/`, `docs/`, `screenshots/`, plus a few stray `tmp_*.jpg` files at the root.

The original `.git` history is preserved in `/tmp/clone-target` if you ever want to reference it; the working tree (minus `.git` and `node_modules`) has been synced into `/home/user/rork-app`.

A couple of quick notes / cleanups I'd suggest:
- The root-level `tmp_img1.jpg`, `tmp_img2.jpg`, `tmp_screenshot1.jpg`, `tmp_screenshot2.jpg` (≈5.8 MB total) look like leftover scratch files — want me to remove them?
- The original repo had `node_modules` excluded from the clone, so you'll need to run `bun install` in `expo/` (and `backend/` if it has its own) before anything runs.

What would you like to do next — open one of the apps to inspect/build it, clean up the stray files, or something else?

responseMessageContents.title: Import of the real estate investment project from GitHub.
responseMessageContents.description: The project files from the GitHub repository were successfully copied into the workspace. This includes the mobile apps and the backend system so you can start working on them.
